candidate_llm_application: SONGLINE-CURRICULUM: graph-traversal-ordered continual-learning training schedule. Training corpus pre-clustered into waypoint graph G=(V,E) with topic-embedding + hierarchical-difficulty d_v in {1..D} + prerequisite-edges. Training proceeds BFS-from-novice-frontier; advance to d=k+1 only after BFS-frontier coverage of d=k exceeds threshold tau. Periodic 'inma replay' revisits sampled completed waypoints.
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL

results.title: CL Pretraining 2601.21698 — 200-model systematic with 6 difficulty metrics
results.rationale: Systematic curriculum-pretraining with hierarchical difficulty metrics directly corresponds to the hierarchical-difficulty d_v annotation + difficulty-ordered training schedule that is the core of SONGLINE-CURRICULUM. Multi-metric difficulty scoring is the same selection mechanism modulo waypoint-graph framing.

results.title: CAMPUS 2509.13790 — multi-metric difficulty sort + per-step competence + sub-curriculum selection
results.rationale: EXACT TWIN — competence-aware step-by-step sub-curriculum advancement gated by current competence is identical in mechanism to BFS-from-novice-frontier advancing to d=k+1 only after tau coverage of d=k. Multi-metric difficulty + per-step competence gating IS the songline-walk; cultural framing is the only differentiator.

results.title: Beyond Random Sampling Curriculum 2506.11300 — curriculum-ordered pretraining sample selection
results.rationale: Non-random curriculum-ordered sample selection is the trajectory-ordered training-data presentation principle. Same effect: present training data in difficulty-ordered traversal rather than i.i.d. shuffle.

results.title: Curriculum-Guided Layer Scaling 2506.11389 — progressive difficulty escalation
results.rationale: Progressive difficulty escalation tied to model capacity growth is functionally equivalent to advancing through hierarchical difficulty levels d=1..D. Same monotonic-difficulty-progression schedule.

results.title: CER ICLR 2025 — experience replay in context window for CL
results.rationale: Periodic experience replay for continual learning is functionally the 'inma replay revisits sampled completed waypoints' component. Replay over completed samples is the same anti-forgetting mechanism.

results.title: Scalable CL Replay 2505.12512 — replay-ratio consolidation + post-task training
results.rationale: Replay-ratio consolidation across completed tasks IS the periodic revisit of completed waypoints. The waypoint-graph framing is novel terminology, but the underlying sampled-replay schedule is identical.

results.title: CL Survey 2603.12658 — CPT/DAP/CFT taxonomy
results.rationale: Comprehensive continual-learning taxonomy covering ordered-presentation + replay schedules subsumes the proposed BFS-from-novice + inma-replay schedule as a combination of taxonomy-established mechanisms.

results.title: Waypoint-Trajectory LLM Planner — LLM suggests intermediate waypoints for trajectory
results.rationale: Waypoint-graph + trajectory traversal vocabulary is already deployed for LLM planning; mapping the same graph-traversal formalism onto training-data ordering is a re-domaining (planning -> curriculum), not a new mechanism. Waypoint and BFS-traversal terminology already canonical.

reason: All 8 search results trigger hit under sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7. CAMPUS 2509.13790 is an exact functional twin: multi-metric difficulty sorting + per-step competence-aware sub-curriculum selection IS the BFS-from-novice-frontier advancement gated by coverage threshold tau. CL Pretraining 2601.21698 (hierarchical difficulty), Beyond Random Sampling 2506.11300 (curriculum-ordered presentation), Layer Scaling 2506.11389 (progressive difficulty), CER + Scalable CL Replay (sampled replay over completed material), CL Survey (taxonomy), and Waypoint-Trajectory LLM Planner (waypoint-graph formalism) jointly cover every component of SONGLINE-CURRICULUM: (a) waypoint-graph annotation, (b) difficulty-ordered traversal, (c) competence-gated advancement, (d) periodic replay of completed waypoints. The tjukurpa/songline framing supplies cultural narrative but no novel computational mechanism beyond competence-aware curriculum + replay-consolidation already standard. total_hits=8 >= 1, candidate FAILS the threshold.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. The proposed graph-traversal-ordered curriculum + inma-replay is fully anticipated by CAMPUS (competence-aware sub-curriculum) + CL Pretraining 2601.21698 (multi-metric difficulty) + Scalable CL Replay (replay consolidation) + Waypoint-Trajectory Planner (waypoint-graph formalism already in LLM literature). To salvage, candidate would need a distinctive mechanism such as (a) sky-and-land dual-axis embedding with cross-axis prerequisite constraints not reducible to single difficulty scalar, (b) ceremonially-encoded explicit graph-as-data-artifact transmission protocol (versioned, signed, transferable across model families with auditable provenance), or (c) age-gated multi-grade simultaneous traversal with role-conditioned masking — not merely BFS-from-novice + scheduled replay, which is competence-aware curriculum + replay-buffer CL already canonical.
